g.data-narration: Welcome to Android Services. We'll explore background operations that keep your app running even when not visible.
A Service is an Android component that performs long-running operations in the background without a user interface.
g.text: An application component that performs long-running operations in the background
without providing a UI. Continues running even when user switches apps.

g.data-narration: There are three main types: Started Service runs indefinitely, Bound Service provides client-server interface, and IntentService handles async requests.
A Started Service launches via startService and runs independently in the background until explicitly stopped.
g.text: Launched by calling startService(). Runs indefinitely in background.
Must stop itself with stopSelf() or be stopped by another component.

g.data-narration: The started service lifecycle shows onCreate when first created, onStartCommand for each start request, and onDestroy for cleanup.
A Bound Service uses bindService to create a client-server interface for component interaction and returns an IBinder.
g.text: Allows components to bind using bindService(). Provides client-server interface
for communication. Runs only while components are bound to it.

g.data-narration: Bound service lifecycle includes onCreate, onBind returning IBinder, optional onUnbind when clients disconnect, and onDestroy.
IntentService is a Service subclass that handles async requests on a worker thread, processing intents one at a time.
g.text: Subclass of Service that handles async requests on a worker thread.
Processes intents one at a time, auto-stops when queue is empty.

g.data-narration: Foreground Services display a notification and have higher priority, making them less likely to be killed by the system.
g.text: Performs noticeable operations with a persistent notification.
Higher priority than background services - less likely to be killed.

g.data-narration: Service lifecycle methods include onCreate for initialization, onStartCommand for each start request, and onBind for client connections.
g.text: Called once when service is first created
Called each time a client starts the service
Called when service is being destroyed

g.data-narration: The onStartCommand return value determines service restart behavior: START_STICKY restarts with null intent, START_NOT_STICKY doesn't restart, START_REDELIVER_INTENT restarts and redelivers the last intent.
Here's a complete started service implementation with onCreate, onStartCommand handling intents on a background thread, and onDestroy cleanup.
g.text: public int onStartCommand(Intent intent, int flags, int startId) {
// Work on background thread, then stopSelf(startId)
